calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Linda Harrington.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that Mr. Turner was paying his expenses on his own and that the bills paid by the Trustee were for the ones that he was not able to cover. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order). The Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel was withdrawn.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel.
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Text Ruling

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brent Kato for Linda Harrington (Trustee).

The Court stated that the accounting filed by Ms. Harrington should be in the form of a petition and that Counsel filed a motion to withdraw. Mr. Kato stated that a supplemental petition was being prepared, that they would ask for termination of supervision of the Trust, that Mr. Turner was ill and most likely would not appear in Court, that Mr. Turner may need a conservatorship and that sanctions had been paid this morning. In addition, Mr. Kato stated that he would speak to Mr. Keith to see if he could assist with getting consent from Mr. Turner. The Court instructed Mr. Kato to get a hearing date of April 12, 2010 for his petition, requested a declaration regarding Mr. Turner's inability to appear and stated that the Trustee's powers are re-instated. All Orders to Show Cause are discharged. All matters are off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brent Kato for Linda Harrington (Trustee).

Mr. Kato stated that he had no contact with Ms. Harrington. The Court expressed concern with regard to the accounting not being filed for ending period of September 30, 2009, with the multiple continuances and with a Professional Fiduciary not communicating with her Attorney. The Order to Show Cause issued on January 6, 2010 informed Ms. Harrington that fines/sanctions would be imposed if the accounting was not filed by today. Therefore, Ms. Harrington is fined in the amount of $250.00 for failure to appear and for failure to file the accounting. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will commence on January 21, 2010 until the accounting is filed. Linda Harrington's powers as Trustee are suspended. Further status was set February 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the accounting is not filed by that date, the sanctions will increase to $50.00 per day. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Trustee Linda Harrington to appear and show why she should not be removed as Trustee, to explain why she has failed to communicate with her Attorney and her roll as a Fiduciary, and why the accounting was not timely filed. The return date is February 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Continued To Jan-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andy Tran for Linda Harrington.

An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Linda Harrington to appear on January 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to: 1) why she failed to file an account for the period ending September 30, 2009 two weeks before December 9, 2009 as previously ordered by Judge Dearman on October 28, 2008. 2) why as a Professional Fiduciary she has not communicated with her Attorney Andy Tran and 3) why her powers as Trusteee should not be revoked. Ms. Harrington is ordered to appear on that date. Failure to appear will result in a finding of contempt and a $250.00 sanction. For each day commencing January 21, 2010 that the accounting is not filed, sanctions will accrue at $25.00 per day.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Continued To Jan-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
